metadata.name: NERC CIP (Critical Infrastructure Protection) Standards
metadata.description: The NERC CIP (North American Electric Reliability Corporation Critical Infrastructure Protection) standards are a set of requirements designed to secure the assets required for operating North America's bulk electric system. Compliance with NERC CIP standards is mandatory for utilities and electricity suppliers in North America.
metadata.classification_reasoning: NERC CIP is a mandatory regulatory framework for electricity sector entities in North America, enforced by NERC with fines for non-compliance.
metadata.framework_purpose: To ensure cybersecurity and physical security of assets required for operating North America's bulk electric system, protecting critical infrastructure from cyber and physical attacks.
metadata.framework_scope: Registered entities within NERC's jurisdiction including utilities, transmission operators, generation operators, reliability coordinators, and others operating Bulk Electric System (BES) assets.
framework_objectives: Protect critical cyber assets supporting the Bulk Electric System
Establish physical security for critical infrastructure
Ensure personnel reliability and security training
Manage supply chain risks for industrial control systems
Implement incident reporting and response for the electricity sector
metadata.target_audience: Utility companies, grid operators, generation facilities, transmission operators, regulatory compliance officers, and OT/ICS security professionals
metadata.regulatory_authority: North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) / Regional Entities
adoption_approach: Identify BES Cyber Systems within scope using CIP-002
Establish personnel training and access management
Implement electronic and physical security perimeters
Develop incident response and recovery plans
Conduct compliance audits and report violations

controls.title: BES Cyber System Identification
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement a process that considers each of the following assets for purposes of categorizing BES Cyber Systems.
controls.full_text: Identify all BES Cyber Systems and their associated BES Cyber Assets. Apply the impact rating criteria (High, Medium, Low) to each BES Cyber System based on the systems they support. Maintain documentation of the categorization rationale.
evidence_requirements: BES Cyber System list with categorizations

controls.title: Cybersecurity Policies
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall review and obtain CIP Senior Manager approval at least once every 15 calendar months for one or more documented cybersecurity policies.
controls.full_text: Develop and maintain cybersecurity policies covering all CIP standards. Policies must be approved by the CIP Senior Manager at least every 15 months. Policies must address all applicable NERC CIP standards. Policies must be communicated to all personnel with access to BES Cyber Systems.

controls.title: Security Awareness Training
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement a documented ongoing cyber security awareness program to reinforce cyber security practices.
controls.full_text: Implement an ongoing cybersecurity awareness program covering all personnel with access to BES Cyber Systems. Cybersecurity training must be completed at least once per calendar year and must include applicable policies, physical and electronic access controls, NERC CIP requirements, and methods of reporting cybersecurity incidents.
evidence_requirements: Training content covering NERC CIP requirements

controls.title: Access Management Program
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement one or more documented access management programs for physical and electronic access.
controls.full_text: Implement access management programs for BES Cyber Systems and Critical Cyber Assets. Manage access authorizations, revoke access timely upon personnel changes, conduct quarterly reviews of Electronic Access Control Lists, and review physical access authorizations at least every 15 months.

controls.title: Electronic Security Perimeter
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement one or more documented Electronic Security Perimeter(s) for all applicable high and medium impact BES Cyber Systems.
controls.full_text: Establish Electronic Security Perimeters (ESPs) around all High and Medium impact BES Cyber Systems. Control all routable communications crossing the ESP boundary. Identify all Electronic Access Points (EAPs) in the ESP. Deny all access by default and allow only specifically authorized communications.

controls.title: Interactive Remote Access Management
controls.description: Each responsible entity allowing Interactive Remote Access must implement and document methods for managing Interactive Remote Access.
controls.full_text: Manage all Interactive Remote Access sessions to BES Cyber Systems. Require MFA for all remote access. Encrypt all remote access sessions. Monitor and log all remote access. Implement session timeout controls. Limit remote access to authorized individuals only.

controls.title: Physical Security Plan
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement one or more documented Physical Security Plan(s) for its applicable high and medium impact BES Cyber Systems.
controls.full_text: Develop and implement physical security plans for BES Cyber Systems. Define Physical Security Perimeters (PSPs). Control and monitor all physical access to PSPs. Use access controls such as badges, biometrics, or guards. Retain physical access logs for at least 90 calendar days.

controls.title: Ports and Services Management
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement one or more documented processes that collectively include each of the applicable requirement parts in CIP-007-6 Table R1.
controls.full_text: Enable only necessary ports and services on BES Cyber Systems. Document all enabled ports and services. Disable or restrict unnecessary ports and services. Review enabled ports and services at least every 35 calendar months. Protect BES Cyber Systems from unintentional or active communications via other protocols.

controls.title: Malicious Code Prevention
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement one or more documented processes for malicious code prevention.
controls.full_text: Deploy and maintain anti-malware protection on all BES Cyber Systems where technically feasible. Update signatures within 35 calendar days of availability. Test mitigation procedures where malware prevention tools cannot be installed. Log malware events and investigate detections.
evidence_requirements: Exception documentation for systems without anti-malware

controls.title: Security Event Monitoring
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement one or more documented processes for security event monitoring.
controls.full_text: Log security events on BES Cyber Systems for at least the last 90 calendar days. Review logs of security events to identify cyber security incidents or potential incidents. Alert designated personnel for specific events requiring immediate response such as detected compromise or attempted unauthorized access.

controls.title: Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall document one or more Cyber Security Incident response plan(s) that collectively include each of the applicable requirement parts in CIP-008-6 Table R1.
controls.full_text: Develop and maintain a Cyber Security Incident response plan for BES Cyber Systems. The plan must include roles and responsibilities, incident classification criteria, response procedures, communication plans, and reporting requirements to NERC E-ISAC and government agencies. Test the plan annually.
evidence_requirements: Incident reporting procedures to NERC E-ISAC

controls.title: Recovery Plans
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall have one or more documented recovery plan(s) for BES Cyber Systems that collectively include each of the applicable requirement parts.
controls.full_text: Develop and maintain recovery plans for restoring BES Cyber Systems following a cyber incident. Recovery plans must include conditions for activation, roles and responsibilities, backup and restoration procedures, and verification testing procedures. Test recovery plans at least once every 15 calendar months.

controls.title: Configuration Change Management
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement one or more documented processes for configuration change management in accordance with the requirements in CIP-010.
controls.full_text: Maintain baseline configurations for all BES Cyber Systems. Document all authorized changes. Verify the identity of hardware components for High impact systems. Authorize all changes and test changes before implementation in the production environment. Revert to baseline if unauthorized changes are detected.

controls.title: Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement one or more documented processes for vulnerability management in accordance with CIP-010-3 R3.
controls.full_text: Conduct vulnerability assessments for BES Cyber Systems at least once every 15 calendar months. Include active vulnerability scanning where technically feasible. Document discovered vulnerabilities. Implement mitigation plans for identified vulnerabilities based on risk.
evidence_requirements: Scanning tool exemptions and compensating controls

controls.title: BES Cyber System Information Protection
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall implement and document one or more information protection program(s) to identify, classify, and protect information associated with BES Cyber Systems.
controls.full_text: Develop and implement a BES Cyber System Information (BCSI) protection program. Identify what constitutes BCSI. Classify and protect BCSI from unauthorized disclosure. Control access to BCSI based on need-to-know. Implement secure handling and disposal procedures for BCSI.

controls.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
controls.description: Each responsible entity shall develop one or more documented supply chain cyber security risk management plan(s) for high and medium impact BES Cyber Systems.
controls.full_text: Develop and implement a supply chain cybersecurity risk management plan for vendors of BES Cyber Systems. Address: vendor risk identification, software integrity verification, vendor remote access controls, and information sharing about known vulnerabilities. Review and obtain approval for the plan at least every 15 calendar months.

controls.title: Transmission Station Physical Security
controls.description: Each applicable Transmission Owner shall perform an initial and subsequent periodic risk assessment of its Transmission stations and Transmission substations to identify those that, if rendered inoperable or damaged, could result in widespread instability, uncontrolled separation, or cascading within an Interconnection.
controls.full_text: Conduct risk assessments of transmission stations and substations to identify those with high criticality to grid reliability. Implement physical security plans for identified critical facilities. Include security patrols, barriers, monitoring systems, and intrusion detection. Engage law enforcement and government agencies in planning.
evidence_requirements: Physical security plans for critical substations

controls.title: NERC CIP Compliance Auditing
controls.description: Responsible entities must maintain evidence of compliance with all applicable CIP standards for audit purposes.
controls.full_text: Maintain comprehensive evidence for all applicable NERC CIP requirements including policies, procedures, training records, access logs, configuration records, and test results. Prepare for periodic NERC/Regional Entity compliance audits. Self-certify compliance annually. Report violations promptly to NERC. Implement mitigation plans for violations.
